🌿 BUILT BY A MAKER FOR MAKERS

Generate print-ready
CLP Classification, Labelling & Packaging labels in seconds

Hi, I'm Michaela — a candle and wax melt maker and the person who built CLPeasy. My background is in IT. I spent a few years working in IT for a Formula 1 team and inside a global enterprise software company. In those environments I learned that the best tools are invisible — they do the hard work so the people using them can focus on what they're actually brilliant at, making products.

When I started making candles and hit the wall of CLP compliance, I couldn't find a tool to easily create the labels for me. So I built one.... CLPeasy. What used to take hours of guesswork now takes minutes — and the £200–£600 a year most makers spend on compliance stays where it belongs: in your craft.

Do I need a CLP label? What UK makers need to know

If you sell fragrance products, a CLP label is the law

⚖️
What is a CLP label?
A CLP label is a legally required hazard label under the Classification, Labelling and Packaging Regulation. If you sell candles, wax melts, reed diffusers, room sprays or any product containing fragrance or essential oils, every product must carry one before it can legally be sold.
🏷️
What does it include?
Your CLP label must show: hazard pictograms (GHS diamonds), a signal word (WARNING or DANGER), H statements, P statements, allergen declarations, a UFI code, and your details as the responsible person. CLPeasy generates all of these automatically from your fragrance supplier's Safety Data Sheet.
🚨
What happens if you don't have one?
Selling without a compliant CLP label is a criminal offence. Trading Standards can issue improvement notices, require products to be withdrawn from sale, and issue fines. Online marketplaces including Etsy and Amazon are also increasingly enforcing CLP compliance.
CLPeasy is currently available for UK makers. EU market support — including multi-language label output for selling into France, Germany, Spain, Italy and the Netherlands — is coming soon. See what's included →
How it works

From SDS to printed label in 3 steps

No design skills needed. No compliance guesswork. Just your fragrance supplier's safety data sheet and 3 minutes.

📄
1

Get your SDS

Download the Safety Data Sheet (SDS) from your fragrance supplier. Every fragrance supplier provides one — it contains all the hazard data needed to build your compliant CLP label.

✨
2

Paste into CLPeasy

Paste your complete SDS document into Smart Paste — or just Section 2.2 if you prefer. CLPeasy automatically finds the hazard classification section and extracts the signal word, GHS pictograms, H statements and allergens. No manual data entry. Always your own current document.

🖨️
3

Download your label

Preview your label in real time, then download as high-res PNG, SVG or a print-ready PDF. Your label is saved to your library instantly — edit or re-download any time.

Features

Everything you need to stay compliant

Built specifically for UK makers who need a legitimate, compliant CLP label on any product they make and sell publicly — not generic label software.

⚡

Supplier library COMING SOON

Select your fragrance supplier and CLPeasy will guide you directly to their SDS page — no hunting around. In the meantime, use Smart Paste with any supplier's SDS PDF from anywhere in the world.

✨

Smart Paste

Paste your complete SDS document — CLPeasy automatically finds Section 2.2 and extracts all hazard data instantly. Works with any supplier's SDS, any fragrance, any update. Your label is always built from your own current document, not a stored database.

💾

Your label library

Every label you create is saved to your account. Access your full library from any device, any time. Never re-enter data twice.

🖨️

Print-ready output

Download high-res PNG for home printing or Cricut. SVG export optimised for Cricut Design Space. A4 PDF print sheets included.

📐

Any size, any shape

Standard sizes — 52mm standard candle (the CLP minimum), 63mm large jar. Need something different? Set your own custom width and height. Circle, square or rectangle.

Compliance stress
Wondering if you got the right H statements. Whether you need EUH208. If your signal word is correct. Trading Standards can fine you if it's wrong.
Cost: priceless — and very real

Every new scent means starting again
New fragrance? New jar size? New packaging? Back to square one. Resize, recheck, reprint. Every. Single. Time.
Cost: hours you could spend making
💸
The total adds up fast
Most small makers spend £150–£1,000+ per year — in tools, designer fees, wasted materials and time — just on CLP labels alone.

Our story

Created by a maker, for makers

I started making candles and wax melts as a hobby and quickly fell in love with it. But when it came to selling my products, the CLP labelling requirements felt overwhelming — reading SDS sheets, decoding H statements, figuring out what pictograms were needed.

I couldn't find a simple, affordable tool that worked the way I needed it to — one that wasn't tied to a single supplier, worked on my phone, and guided me through the process step by step.

So I built CLPeasy™. With 25+ years of IT experience behind me — including roles supporting a Formula 1 team and inside a global enterprise software company — I personally set up, designed, and developed every part of this platform: the website, the label builder, and all the back-end database and email integrations. That means I have full IT control of CLPeasy. No call centres. No lengthy wait times. No having to explain your problem again every time you contact us.

I've also built a service monitoring tool that alerts me the moment anything goes wrong — so if a system goes down, I know about it immediately and can restore it to full working order as efficiently as possible. That's my personal commitment to every CLPeasy customer, whether you're on a free trial or a full subscription.

Before launch, I proactively contacted the HSE CLP Helpdesk to ensure CLPeasy operates responsibly within GB CLP Regulation — because getting this right matters to me as much as it does to you. You can read our full regulatory position on the Compliance & Transparency page.

I hope CLPeasy makes your journey as a maker just a little bit easier.

Massively inspired by Joy Mangano — American inventor, entrepreneur, and proof that the best ideas come from solving your own problems.

— Michaela, Crafty Mouse Gifts

Why CLPeasy is different

After searching for a single tool that could handle CLP compliance from start to finish, I quickly discovered that nothing like this existed. CLPeasy is the only service in the global market built specifically to help fragrance product makers stay legally compliant — with confidence that they are meeting every rule and regulation. We understand the effort, skill, creativity and dedication that goes into creating your products and building your brand. Your labels are the final detail that completes that picture. The smallest things make the biggest impact, and your products deserve to look as professional as they are.

FAQ

Common questions

What is a CLP label and do I legally need one?
Yes — if you sell candles or wax melts in the UK or EU you are legally required to include a CLP label under the Classification, Labelling and Packaging regulation. It must show hazard pictograms, signal word, H and P statements, allergen declarations and your details as the responsible person. Trading Standards can issue fines for non-compliance.
Will my labels be legally compliant?
CLPeasy generates labels based on the SDS data you enter from your fragrance supplier. You must always verify the information against your own SDS sheet at your actual fragrance load before printing and selling. CLPeasy provides the tool — you are responsible for verifying the final label content is accurate for your specific product.
What is an SDS sheet and where do I find mine?
An SDS (Safety Data Sheet) is a document provided by your fragrance supplier that contains all the hazard information for their fragrance oil. You should find it on your supplier's website — usually in a dedicated SDS or safety section. Section 2.2 (Label elements) is the part CLPeasy reads. You can paste the complete SDS document into Smart Paste and CLPeasy will find Section 2.2 automatically — you don't need to locate it yourself. If you can't find your SDS, contact your supplier directly and ask for the SDS at 10% fragrance load.
How does Smart Paste work?
Open your fragrance supplier's SDS PDF and copy all the text — you don't need to find Section 2.2 yourself. Paste the complete document into the Smart Paste box in CLPeasy. The tool automatically locates Section 2.2, reads only the hazard classification data from it, and fills in the H statements, P statements, signal word, pictograms and allergens for you. Your label is always built from your own current SDS — not from a stored database that may not reflect the latest update to your fragrance. Always review what has been extracted before downloading your label.
My supplier isn't in the library — can I still use CLPeasy?
Yes — CLPeasy works with any fragrance supplier, anywhere in the world. Paste your complete SDS document into Smart Paste and CLPeasy extracts all hazard data automatically. You can also enter everything manually using the H statement and P statement libraries. Because CLPeasy reads your own SDS directly rather than looking data up from a stored list, there's no limit on supplier coverage and your data is always current.
What size labels does CLPeasy generate?
CLPeasy includes two standard preset sizes — 52mm circle (the CLP minimum, suits most standard candle jars) and 63mm circle (large jar candle). These cover the majority of candle products. If your product is smaller (such as a wax melt clamshell) or a different shape entirely, you can set a fully custom width and height in millimetres — the compliance warning will guide you if you go below the 52mm CLP minimum. CLPeasy also supports three shapes — circle, square and rectangle — so whatever label you need, you can create it.
How do I use my label in Cricut Design Space?
Download your label as an SVG file from CLPeasy. In Cricut Design Space, click Upload → Upload Image → select your SVG file. Resize it to your exact label dimensions (e.g. 50mm × 50mm). Use the Print Then Cut feature for best results — Cricut will print your label and then cut it to shape automatically.
Does CLPeasy work on mobile?
Yes — CLPeasy is fully mobile friendly and works on iPhone and Android. You can build, preview and download labels directly from your phone. PNG files save to your camera roll and SVG files save to your files app.
What is EUH208 and do I need it on my label?
EUH208 is a supplementary hazard statement that means "Contains [substance name] — may produce an allergic reaction." You need it on your label if your fragrance contains a CLP/REACH classified skin sensitiser above the threshold concentration (0.01% for category 1A, 0.1% for category 1B). Your SDS Section 2.2 will tell you exactly which sensitisers are present and whether EUH208 applies. CLPeasy has a built-in library of all 28 common CLP fragrance sensitisers to help you.
Can I cancel my subscription at any time?
Yes — cancel anytime with no questions asked. Your labels remain saved in your account and you keep full Pro access until the end of your current billing period.
What's the difference between Easy Trial, Easy Start and Easy Pro?
Easy Trial is free for 14 days with access to every feature and up to 10 watermarked downloads — no card needed. After the trial, Easy Start (£9.99/mo or £99/year) gives you 20 downloads/month, no watermark, the full label builder, supplier SDS guide, A4 print sheets and Cricut export. Easy Pro (£14.99/mo or £149/year) gives you 30 downloads/month and adds SDS Smart Import (AI paste), UFI generator, QR digital safety sheets, batch export and label folders. Both plans include top-up packs if you need more — 5 extra downloads for £3.99 or 10 for £7.99. Not sure which plan is right for you? Try our 5-question plan finder →
Are there any download limits?
The Easy Trial includes up to 10 watermarked label downloads so you can test the full workflow. Easy Start gives you 20 downloads/month and Easy Pro gives you 30 downloads/month — shown as a progress bar in the builder so you always know where you stand. If you need more in any month, top-up packs are available: 5 extra downloads for £3.99 or 10 for £7.99. Credits never expire and stack on top of your monthly allowance.

CLPeasy™ Setup Guide
Step-by-step guide for every plan
✓ Free for 14 days — no card needed
Full access to every feature. Up to 10 watermarked label downloads so you can test the complete workflow before committing.
1Create your account
Click Start free trial on the home or pricing page. Enter your name, email and a password. No card details needed. You'll receive a verification email straight away.
📧 A verification email will arrive in your inbox — click the link to activate your account
2Open the label builder
Once verified, sign in and you'll land in the builder. Choose your label shape and size on Step 1. Circle presets cover most candle and wax melt jars. Rectangle presets cover reed diffuser wraps, room sprays and snap bars.
3Add your hazard data
On Step 3 (Hazards), use Smart Paste — copy your complete SDS PDF text, paste it into the box and click Extract hazard data. CLPeasy finds Section 2.2 automatically and fills in the signal word, pictograms, H statements, P statements and allergens.
💡 You don't need to find Section 2.2 yourself — paste the whole document and CLPeasy does the rest. Works with any supplier worldwide.
4Enter your business details
On Step 4, enter your brand name, address, phone number and optional website. These are required on every CLP label as the responsible person details. CLPeasy saves them for next time.
5Preview and download
Step 5 shows your finished label in real time. Trial labels include a small CLPeasy watermark. Download as PNG, SVG, JPEG or PDF. Your label is saved to your library automatically.
📸 Step 5 — label preview & download
screenshot coming soon
💡 Subscribe at any time to remove the watermark. Your library and all saved labels carry over immediately.
🕯️ Easy Start — £9.99/month or £99/year
20 downloads/month · No watermark · PNG, SVG, JPEG, PDF · A4 print sheets · Cricut export · Label library · QR safety sheets
1Choose your plan
Visit the Pricing page, select Easy Start and choose Monthly (£9.99/mo) or Annual (£99/yr — save 30%). Click Get started.
2Complete checkout via Stripe
You'll be taken to a secure Stripe checkout page. Enter your card details and click Pay and subscribe. Your subscription activates immediately and you'll be redirected back to the builder.
🔒 Payments are processed securely by Stripe. CLPeasy never stores your card details.
3Build labels without a watermark
Your watermark is removed immediately and 20 downloads are available. All labels from your trial are still in your library — nothing is lost.
📸 Builder — subscribed view
screenshot coming soon
4Download in any format
Easy Start unlocks all download formats — high-res PNG for home printing, SVG for Cricut Design Space, JPEG, and PDF print sheets with multiple labels per A4.
💡 For Cricut: download SVG → upload to Cricut Design Space → resize to your label dimensions → use Print Then Cut.
5Manage your account
Visit My account to view your subscription, download usage, next billing date, and to cancel or upgrade at any time.
⚡ Easy Pro — £14.99/month or £149/year
Everything in Easy Start + Smart Paste · UFI generator · QR digital safety sheets · SDS update alerts · Batch export
1Subscribe to Easy Pro
Visit Pricing, select Easy Pro and complete checkout via Stripe — same process as Easy Start.
2Use Smart Paste PRO
On Step 3 of the builder, click Smart Paste. Open your supplier's SDS PDF, select all text (Ctrl+A / Cmd+A), copy and paste into the box. CLPeasy extracts everything automatically — signal word, pictograms, H & P statements, allergens. No manual entry needed.
💡 Works with any supplier, any fragrance, worldwide. CLPeasy finds Section 2.2 automatically from the full document.
3Generate your UFI PRO
On Step 3, expand the UFI Code section and click Generate. CLPeasy creates a unique formula identifier — required on all CLP labels sold in the UK and EU. It's saved with your label automatically.
📸 UFI generator
screenshot coming soon
4Add a QR safety sheet PRO
On Step 5, toggle QR code on. CLPeasy generates a permanent digital safety sheet and adds a scannable QR code to your label. The link stays live even if you cancel — because it may already be printed on products in customers' hands.
📸 QR toggle — Step 5 preview
screenshot coming soon
5SDS update alerts PRO
CLPeasy sends an automatic SDS review reminder at 11 months so you're always prompted to check for supplier updates. Stay current, stay compliant, never get caught out by an outdated label.
Need more downloads?
Top-up packs available on both plans — 5 extra downloads for £3.99 or 10 for £7.99. Credits never expire and stack on top of your monthly allowance.
